data.title: TNMP Files Comprehensive Rate Settlement
data.description: On May 29, 2026, in Albuquerque, New Mexico, TNMP, a subsidiary of TXNM Energy (NYSE: TXNM) operating in Texas, submitted a detailed settlement in its base rate review with the Public Utility Commission of Texas (PUCT). The agreement is crucial for TNMP's ongoing operations and future developments.

data.title: Steakholder Foods Ltd. Announces Warrant Exercise for $1.1 Million in Gross Proceeds
data.description: In Rehovot, Israel, on May 29, 2026, Steakholder Foods Ltd. (Nasdaq: STKH), a pioneer in alternative proteins and 3D printing, revealed agreements for the immediate exercise of certain outstanding warrants. These warrants allow the purchase of 892,854 of the Company's American Depository Shares (ADSs), with each ADS representing 4,000 ordinary shares. Originally issued on October 1, 2025, the warrants had an exercise price of $5.00 per ADS, now reduced to $1.25 per ADS. The Company is also set to issue new warrants. The closing of this transaction is anticipated around June 1, 2026, pending standard closing conditions.

data.title: Allied Gold Advances Receipt of Regulatory Approvals and Clearances in Connection with the Proposed Arrangement with Zijin Gold International and Extends the Outside Date to July 29, 2026
data.description: Allied Gold has confirmed that they have received all necessary regulatory approvals and clearances in relation to the proposed arrangement with Zijin Gold International. This development brings the total number of approvals received to X, marking a key milestone in the process. The arrangement between Allied Gold and Zijin Gold International is set to proceed as planned, with the companies now awaiting finalization of Y additional approvals before completion. This news underscores the progress made in sealing the deal and positions them well for the next steps in the partnership.

data.title: Announcement from Eimskip: Strike of bosuns and able seamen cancelled
data.description: Eimskip, representing P/f Faroe Ship, along with the Icelandic Seamens' Union (SÍ), have reached an agreement to call off the strike involving bosuns and able seamen on the vessels Brúarfoss owned by the Company.

data.title: Leica Biosystems and CellCarta Expand Collaborative Companion Diagnostic Development Model to Accelerate Commercialization Globally
data.description: Crafted to accommodate a range of biopharma development requirements, this partnership is driven by strong collaboration and simultaneous implementation. The initiative unveils a versatile international structure for developing companion diagnostics (CDx) aimed at streamlining the transition from initial assays to diagnostic products for commercial use.

data.title: Say Hello to Gigolo: Lisa Vanderpump’s Bold New Cocktail Lounge is Now Open Inside The Vanderpump Hotel
data.description: In Las Vegas, the new cocktail destination Gigolo has been launched at The Vanderpump Hotel by Lisa Vanderpump. The venue is described as a mix of moody and mysterious with a touch of sexy sophistication. Named after Vanderpump's dog, Gigolo, the hotel offers high-quality images for the cocktail lounge and rooms, as well as b-roll footage and soundbites of Lisa Vanderpump discussing the hotel. This new venture adds to the allure of The Vanderpump Hotel and continues Vanderpump's legacy in the hospitality industry.

data.title: Jamie Dimon Blasts Brian Armstrong Over CLARITY Act: 'You Are Full Of...'
data.description: JPMorgan Chase's CEO Jamie Dimon expressed strong opposition to the CLARITY Act in a recent interview, criticizing Coinbase's CEO Brian Armstrong for his lobbying efforts. Dimon specifically highlighted concerns regarding stablecoin yield, arguing that the bill would permit crypto firms to offer rewards to customers for holding stablecoins, creating unfair competition without the necessary consumer protections required of banks. Additionally, Dimon raised issues with the Act's Anti-Money Laundering and Bank Secrecy Act provisions, deeming them insufficient. He emphasized that allowing cryptocurrency firms to pay interest on deposits without adequate protection is unacceptable to banks. Despite Dimon's objections, the full article can be found on Benzinga.com.
